name: Stylish 3-bed flat, indoor pool
host_about: Nous sommes une famille anglo-irlandaise qui ont vécu à La Rochelle depuis 2003. Nous gérons l'hébergement de vacances à La Rochelle, une superbe ville pour les touristes et ceux qui cherchent à s'éloigner de l'agitation de la vie. Aujourd'hui nous passons plusieurs mois chaque année à Londres ou nous gérons aussi un appartement dans un emplacement idéal 20 minutes par métro du centre-ville et loué pour les locations à courte durée. En outre, nous sommes des musiciens et nous chantons, écrivons et jouons ensemble dans un groupe de folk moderne, The Portraits.  We are an Anglo-Irish family who have been living in La Rochelle since 2003. We manage holiday accommodation here, in a super town for tourists and those seeking to get away from the bustle of life. We also spend several months each year in London where we manage another apartment which we rent short term. In addition, we are musicians and we sing, write and play together in a band called The Portraits.

name: Tranquil Room near City Centre
host_about: My name is Joanne. I am from New Zealand and have been living in the UK for over thirty years, living in London for 15 years before settling here in Bristol for twenty years now.   In 2013  I joined the Airbnb Community offering homestay accommodation to travellers & visitors, university students, those looking to relocate and or work in Bristol for short stay visits.   My previous experience of homestay hosting involved working closely with an established Bristol English Language School, 2010 -2013. I was valued by them for my contribution to homestay experiences offered to their foreign language students.   With the unprecedented events of the 2020 Covid 19 Pandemic, I closed my doors to hosting from March 2020 until   June 2021. The three lockdown phases, in particular, though challenging at times, offered me & I hope others some extraordinary experiences, a time to pause, reflect, restore, regroup. So much to fathom during them & in the uncertain times we now continue to face. Gentle, gentle.   It was with some reticence I concede, that I opened my doors to hosting again in the last weeks of June 2021. I am rediscovering again the enriching experience of sharing my home, creating a welcoming, relaxed & comfortable experience for others, a space where connecting in good conversation is valued & respected as much as the need for privacy & discretion.   I feel immense gratitude for the experiences I have shared & been privilege to with guests through Airbnb hosting & I look forward to the many more experiences to come my way.  With much warmth & appreciation, Joanne

name: Stylish 2 bed apartment heart of Clifton Village
Self contained studio with parking in Bristol3
Contemporary Home in Friendly Easton, Free Parking
host_about: We feel our place is an ideal base from which to explore this fabulous city. It’s a short hop to the city centre (ask for a three-stop hop on any local bus and you’ll be in Cabot Circus in no time). We have free on-street parking right outside our door, so you don’t have to worry about pay and display or the parking permits which now place restrictions on motorists in many other parts of the city. If you don’t have a car, that’s no problem, as there is a regular bus into town that stops on our street, or you can take the train from Stapleton Road station and be at Temple Meads or Clifton in no time. If you like a good walk, you can get to Broadmead in around 40 minutes by following the Bristol and Bath Railway Path into town. However, if you want to feel like a real Bristolian, the preferred way to get about Britain’s first ‘cycling city’ is by bike, of course. Our house is handily located next to the Bristol and Bath Railway Path, so why not bring your bikes? The ride to Bath through the Avon Valley is stunningI am a Bristolian born and raised; my wife and I met here at university. Bristol really is a beautiful city. We love taking walks around the Floating Harbour, ensuring we take time to stop for coffee at the Olive Shed, a swift half at the Nova Scotia Hotel, or maybe even a delicious bite to eat at the containers on Wapping Wharf. We also enjoy taking in the stunning views from the top of Brandon Hill before checking out the quirky, artsy shops on Park Street. We always like to look in at the Boston Tea Party on Park Street, where the ‘secret garden’ provides perfect respite from the hustle and bustle of the city. We’ve owned our house in Easton since July 2019. Recently voted one of the top 50 coolest neighbourhoods in the world by TimeOut Magazine (September 2019), Easton really does have something for everyone. You will find trendy cafés selling excellent coffee and home-made cakes, world-beating curry restaurants, and plenty of friendly local boozers. We like to visit the gorgeous Este Kitchen for coffee and brunch. Just opposite, the Greenbank pub is a great spot for lunch or an evening pint or two; their guest ales change all the time and they have a decent selection of board games. If you’re planning a quiet night in, G Bros Pizza will do you a perfectly baked pizza. The M’Sala Library truly is one of the best curries I’ve ever eaten; be sure to ask for the vegetable samosas which are to die for. For the discerning foodie, the famous Bristol Sweet Mart on St Mark’s Road is a five-minute walk away. There you’ll find ingredients for sale from all four corners of the globe. Worth a trip, even just for a browse. We look forward to hosting you soon in beautiful BS5. Chris, Justine and baby Louis.

name: Large penthouse flat, spectacular view
host_about: A keen ecologist, teacher, videographer and adventurer. Our home in St Andrews is a retreat for us - quiet and spacious, combining modern technology (WIFI, Smart TV, central heating) with our love of retro furniture and art. The back of the flat overlooks our garden and Bristol city (as I'm atop a hill) and is extremely tranquil. Yet out the front we can leave our front door and be in central Bristol in under 10 minutes. There are plenty of local bars, award winning restaurants, deli's, and cafes here. Stokes Croft and its vibrant night life, or Gloucester Road ( longest independent high street in Europe) are less than a 10 minute walk. As a new Mum I can also attest to the many great spaces and places for babies and young children in Bristol. If you are planning a family trip message me and I can fill you in. Hope you enjoy Bristol whilst you are here as much as we do :D

name: Bohemian Georgian Luxury in Centre
host_about: I'm Jess and I live with my husband, Sam, our three children, and my mother-in-law... who is also the landlady!  This is her home, and it will be yours too for the duration of your stay.  You will find it to be a most beautiful and magical place to spend time.  This is an open home and we are artists, designers, musicians, cooks, travellers, and writers. The house is a reflection of our creativity and lust for life.  As a family, Sam and I took the children back-packing through Europe and Airbnb was a fantastic way to stay in wonderful places on our shoestring budget.  Now it's our turn to return the favour to others.  We love meeting new people, and welcome you and your stories to fill our home.    As hosts, we can be on hand if you'd like a hands-on experience, or we'll leave you alone if you'd like some space!

name: Urban Paradise
host_about: I'm Jess and I live with my husband, Sam, our three children, and my mother-in-law... who is also the landlady!  This is her home, and it will be yours too for the duration of your stay. name: City View - Sarah's double room.

name: Private room in ‘Garden Square' Georgian townhouse
host_about: We're a busy, active family of two lawyers, two children (post-university age), a lively (13 year old) and a frenetic new born (in July 2025) working cocker spaniel. Both keen skiers/ski tourers and open water swimmers, Anne is a runner and Iain a mountain-biker. There are more bikes in the garage than people in the house ...  PS it’s ‘iain’, not ‘Lain’!

name: Central location, perfect for visitors
host_about: Friendly, considerate and kind, laidback and happy and comfortable with people of all orientations and cultures.  I let my guests have their own space and time. If you need help then I am very happy to assist, if you'd rather be left alone to do your own thing, I totally understand.  I love travelling and have been fortunate enough to visit many different places. I love reading, the opera and theatre, nature and walking my lovely dog.

name: Family home in central Bristol
host_about: We live in Bristol - European green capital in 2015!  I travel quite a bit through work, but also work from home, running an educational business with my wife. We work hard, and like to travel when we can.   As a guest, I'm a relaxed person, and we'd be respectful of your home.   What else do you want to know? Ask...

name: Luxury Apartment with Roof Garden & 360 City Views
host_about: We've lived in Bristol for 20 years and are able to give you lots of suggestions on how to enjoy the city and surrounding areas. Whether this be personal recommendations on visitor attractions or the best cafe's, bars, restaurants & clubs dependant on your taste & budget. We’ve hosting on AirBnB for over 5 years and have stayed as a guest on various travels so appreciate the requirements guests are seeking.  The apartment is always well looked after and cleaned thoroughly with fresh linen.

name: Double room in excellent Clifton location
host_about: I am a very active person competing in triathlons and also horse events, work just about fits in around that! So when I travel and use Airbnb it is normally for sporting related events. When people stay in my Airbnb they rarely see me as I am often out and about!

name: Bristol studio Red Lion Hill Den kitchen+parking
host_about: A wife, mother of two and the CEO of a wee digital agency and tech social enterprise here in Bristol which I run with my husband Stuart. We don't get out much  - the business is all-consuming so we're really looking for some decent short breaks to make up for it :).

name: Comfortable and Clean Flat - Great Location
host_about: I sell mid century furniture and homeware and I love living in Bristol.  I enjoy all the theatre, art and culture that the city has to offer.  I love the food scene in Bristol so have plenty of local information regarding markets and restaurants.  I also enjoy the fact that we have some of the best arts centres in the country and a fabulous local independent neighbourhood.

name: Sophie & Dan's place with Kedi the cat!
host_about: I enjoy travelling and am always busy. In my spare time I like to climb, go to the pub, go camping & travel in our campervan. I am also interested in cooking & enjoy making things.

name: Two double rooms with kitchenette in Totterdown
host_about: We moved to Bristol in 2016 after a couple of years in London. Before that, we lived in Sri Lanka, where Suresh is from, and also Penang, Malaysia. We love meeting new people from around the world and look forward to welcoming you in our home. Combined, we're into cooking, walking, knitting, gym, gardening, films, current affairs, ironing, languages, cycling, singing…

name: 5* Hotel Quality Living - 15mins from City
host_about: My favourite life's saying is "if you change the way you look at things, the things you look at will change".  I'm a positive person and love helping people that's why I'm a Natural Healthcare Practitioner as I get a real sense of glory watching people transform their health and get better and feel better.  My 3 children keep me busy as does my job and when I'm not doing that, I'm on airbnb trying to create lots of lovely experiences with friends and family.  I love just going out and socialising but also cuddling up on the sofa at home with the kids.  Life's about balance!!

My home and business are both in Paintworks - Bristol's creative quarter - which means I'm close by if you need any help or have any problems. host_about: I'm an Art Technician, lecturer and sometime designer, which occasionally leaves time for making, dancing, ranting about Tories etc. I like people, live music, cats, sci-fi, tea, and recovering from covid...  I now host with my partner Tom, who is great at cryptic crosswords and also works in education, previously journalism. He can't dance but he likes a good board game.  Tom and I speak some Spanish and have mostly forgotten how to speak French, German and Japanese, but can stumble through a conversation if prompted :)

name: 'The Sunset Room' Peaceful, Clear Home
host_about: So far I've really enjoyed the experience of hosting the varied and interesting selection of people who have visited my home. I'm calm and friendly, respectful, easy to get along with person. When I travel, I like to stay in places that offer me privacy and space but helpful and friendly if I need anything. I like a clean, clear space, that I can call my own, so I aim to offer the same to my guests, I'm also open to a friendly chat and cuppa if you need that. I'm told people feel relaxed and at ease in my company. I am well traveled including time spent in West Africa and Europe. I've lived in Central and South America and the Caribbean. I love the arts and culture, I'm a practicing writer, photographer and therapist. I love music and dancing and for many years I worked alongside world music artists, working and traveling with artists from all over the world; I think this has given me a broad understanding, appreciation and acceptance of different people and different customs. I relate well to others and am told by people who have limited grasp of the english language that my spoken english and diction is clear and easy to understand. I speak a little spanglish! I like walking, nature and yoga.

name: Large twin room in Shirehampton, Bristol
host_about: I have lived in Shirhampton since June 2019, I share the house with  my best friend and lodger, my 2 children, 3 cats and 12 rescue hens. I have a background in hospitality and take pride in providing a pleasant place to stay. Where possible my impact on the environment is minimised.

name: Lovely light attic room

name: Tranquil Double Room near City Centre
host_about: My name is Joanne. I am from New Zealand and have been living in the UK for over thirty years, living in London for 15 years before settling here in Bristol for twenty years now.   In 2013  I joined the Airbnb Community offering homestay accommodation to travellers & visitors, university students, those looking to relocate and or work in Bristol for short stay visits.   My previous experience of homestay hosting involved working closely with an established Bristol English Language School, 2010 -2013. I was valued by them for my contribution to homestay experiences offered to their foreign language students.   With the unprecedented events of the 2020 Covid 19 Pandemic, I closed my doors to hosting from March 2020 until   June 2021. The three lockdown phases, in particular, though challenging at times, offered me & I hope others some extraordinary experiences, a time to pause, reflect, restore, regroup. So much to fathom during them & in the uncertain times we now continue to face. Gentle, gentle.   It was with some reticence I concede, that I opened my doors to hosting again in the last weeks of June 2021. I am rediscovering again the enriching experience of sharing my home, creating a welcoming, relaxed & comfortable experience for others, a space where connecting in good conversation is valued & respected as much as the need for privacy & discretion.   I feel immense gratitude for the experiences I have shared & been privilege to with guests through Airbnb hosting & I look forward to the many more experiences to come my way.  With much warmth & appreciation, Joanne

It had been converted into offices back in the 1950’s and transforming it into a home suitable for family-living was no small task, especially at it’s a Listed Building with lots of beautiful features. The main house is currently rented out as it makes more sense for us to live closer to Rhian’s parents in Monmouth for now, however we kept the basement separate to use as a crash-pad. name: Colourful Edwardian family home
host_about: Hi there,  I am British and live in Bristol UK with my wife Matilde, who is Swiss-Italian but has lived here in UK since 1976, she speaks Italian and French as well as English.  We have two grown up children and three grand-daughters of 8, 6 and 5 who we are mad about.  We love to travel and here in Europe that usually means our 6 metre Adria camper van.   When the kids left home we had an adult gap year and travelled through Europe to Turkey then back to Morocco, Senegal and Gambia, crossing the Sahara desert twice in our old Mercedes camper.  Then a few of years ago we took the whole family to Southern India for a month which was amazing (I spent a year there in the 80's and hadn't been back since).  So anywhere new pushes all our buttons and we love to explore, meet new people, eat, drink, walk, cycle and generally enjoy what the world has to offer.  We also love music and play piano and accordion (Matilde) and guitar and banjo (me).  So there you are, we look forward to meeting you and enjoying your home and city, wherever it may be.  Now we are hosts too and if you book our lovely home we hope you enjoy staying here and exploring Bristol and the West of England.
